on record to ascertain the correctness of the finding of the 
guilt. The High Court noted that the panch witness had been 
consistent as regards his statement about the appellant having 
accepted the tainted money. In this context, the Court observed 
that if the appellant had not demanded excess Rs. 2/-, there 
was no occasion to accept it in the first place. 
72 
Supreme Court Reports
3.6 As regards the recovery of GC notes – whether those were 
recovered from possession of the appellant or from the 
register lying on his table, the Court observed that the wash 
of the appellant’s hand did turn pink, and both the GC notes 
were found, even if not directly from the appellant, then too 
certainly the notes were found placed on the register. Further, 
the testimony of the Raid Officer, that it was the panch witness 
who pointed out that money accepted by the appellant was lying 
on his register, was found to be reliable and trustworthy. The
